algorithms.title: General Field Approach When Maltreatment Is a Concern
algorithms.description: General, educational sequence for handling a pediatric call where findings raise concern for possible maltreatment. Not a substitute for local mandatory-reporting law, agency policy, or child protective services training.
steps: Provide standard medical care for the child's presenting condition without delay.
Note any inconsistency between the stated mechanism, the injury pattern, and the child's developmental capability.
Note any history that changes between tellings or between different caregivers.
Note any unexplained delay in seeking care.
Maintain calm, professional, non-accusatory communication with caregivers throughout.
Document objectively: exact quotes, observed findings, and scene details — avoid speculative conclusions.
Communicate findings factually during receiving-facility handoff.
Report reasonable suspicion through the appropriate channel per local mandatory-reporting law and agency policy, involving medical direction as indicated.
